title: Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century
summary: The repository artifact copies University of Michigan CME item CME00012, an edition-level selection from George Henslow's 1899 Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century. The encoded selection comprises the title page and printed pp. 1-73 and 76-145: four discontiguous manuscript-derived sections from Henslow's privately held manuscript A, British Library Harley MS 2378, Sloane MS 2584, and Sloane MS 521. It omits the printed preface and introduction, pp. 74-75, the plant list on pp. 147-278, and the index. A complete Royal College of Physicians copy of the 1899 volume is cached. Only one printed photographic specimen of manuscript A and one commercially licensed, unidentified page of Harley MS 2378 were found publicly; no complete public facsimile was found for any of the four physical witnesses, and the current location of manuscript A remains unknown.
review_notes: The cataloged unit is the U-M/General Corpus edited selection, not the complete 1899 volume and not a single medieval work or codex.

Printed pp. 74-75 are absent from the XML even though they lie between manuscript sections A and B; source coverage is therefore recorded as title page, pp. 1-73, and pp. 76-145 rather than pp. 1-145.
Witness descriptions, printed editorial layers, digital encoding, repository artifact, and image surrogates are modeled as distinct entities.
Dates attributed by Henslow and Skeat to the selected material are retained as historical scholarly claims and not substituted for current manuscript-catalog dating.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00012
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify the file as CME00012.
The CME submodule is a distinct repository; this manifest does not alter its source artifact.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00012
entities.description: A publicly accessible digital text encoding the 1899 title page and four manuscript sections while preserving printed page breaks, manuscript page or folio milestones, prose, verse, and Henslow/Skeat notes.

date_statements.label: repository XML structural review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD date documents a review of this serialization, not necessarily the first online publication of the U-M item.

notes: The U-M item states that the material is in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
The digital text's catalog boundary is narrower than the complete Henslow volume.

entities.label: Henslow, Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century (1899)
alternate_labels: Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century, Together with a List of Plants Recorded in Contemporary Writings, with Their Identifications
entities.description: George Henslow's printed compilation and transcription of Middle English material from four manuscripts, with an explanatory introduction and notes by Walter W. Skeat and a separate plant list not included in CME00012.

bibliographic.citation: George Henslow, Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century, Together with a List of Plants Recorded in Contemporary Writings, with Their Identifications, with an explanatory introduction and notes by Walter W. Skeat (London: Chapman and Hall, Limited, 1899).
bibliographic.title: Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century, Together with a List of Plants Recorded in Contemporary Writings, with Their Identifications

notes: CME00012 encodes the title page and printed pp. 1-73 and 76-145 only.
The complete volume's preface, Skeat's explanatory introduction, pp. 74-75, plant list on pp. 147-278, and index are outside the cataloged unit.
James Britten reviewed the excluded plant list; he is not assigned responsibility for the cataloged selection.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Royal College of Physicians copy of Henslow 1899
entities.description: A complete 306-page downloadable scan of the physical 1899 volume contributed by the Royal College of Physicians of London.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete color page-image scan with downloadable PDF and IIIF Presentation manifest
notes: The complete scan is cached locally; CME00012 occupies a discontiguous portion within it.

entities.label: Henslow manuscript A
entities.description: A vellum medical and technical commonplace book owned by George Henslow in 1899. Henslow printed the English material found discontinuously between manuscript pp. 26 and 258. Its present location and complete modern catalog identity were not established.

date_statements.label: Henslow and Skeat dating
date_statements.notes: A historical editorial estimate, not a verified current catalog date.

date_statements.label: Middle English Dictionary source dating
date_statements.notes: The MED source citation conflicts with the earlier Henslow/Skeat estimate; the conflict is preserved rather than resolved.

physical_description.format: Quarto commonplace book in contemporary wooden boards covered with leather
notes: Henslow reports that most of the book is in one hand and that some initials are illuminated.
The description is historical and has not been checked against the physical manuscript.
Henslow says one of his sons found the manuscript in the library of the late J. Johnstone and gave it to him.
No public evidence found in this review justifies describing the witness as lost or destroyed.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2378
entities.description: An English composite miscellany of medical, culinary, and alchemical texts and recipes. Henslow's section B selects material between the manuscript's legacy pages 5 and 324.

date_statements.label: medical and culinary recipe component, ff. 155r-168v
date_statements.notes: The component includes two recipes printed by Henslow on pp. 120-122; this date is not assigned automatically to every selected passage in section B.

holding.notes: The current catalog records modern foliation and an older pagination used by Henslow.

physical_description.support: Parchment, with paper leaves at the beginning
physical_description.extent: vi paper leaves and 184 manuscript leaves
physical_description.format: Composite codex assembled from multiple production units
notes: Multiple scribes and production units are represented.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 2584
entities.description: A 117-folio multilingual medical and recipe miscellany. Henslow's section C selects Middle English material between the manuscript's legacy pages 5 and 180.

date_statements.label: medieval medical and recipe portions
date_statements.notes: The current record describes a composite range extending into the seventeenth century; this narrower wording applies only to the medieval portions relevant to Henslow's selection.

notes: The current record describes English, Dutch, French, and Latin material across the full composite object.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 521
entities.description: A thick multilingual composite medical manuscript. Henslow's section D selects Middle English passages from ff. 200r-272r.

date_statements.label: selected Middle English portion according to Skeat
date_statements.notes: A historical editorial estimate for the selected portion; the current catalog describes the composite manuscript as containing material from the tenth through eighteenth centuries.

physical_description.extent: at least 275 folios in the cataloged contents
physical_description.dimensions: Henslow describes a small, thick volume approximately 5 x 4 inches
notes: The dimensions are Henslow's historical report and have not been checked against a current codicological description.

entities.label: Printed photographic specimen of Henslow manuscript A, manuscript p. 26
entities.description: The single manuscript photograph printed as the frontispiece to Henslow's 1899 volume, showing the opening of the English dyeing material on manuscript p. 26.
entities.reproduction_format: One black-and-white printed photographic plate, digitized in the Internet Archive scan
notes: This is a one-page partial surrogate, not a facsimile of the complete physical manuscript.

entities.label: Bridgeman Images asset BL3708374 from Harley MS 2378
entities.description: A commercially licensed image of one unidentified page from Harley MS 2378. The provider title identifies the manuscript but does not give a folio, so overlap with Henslow's selected passages is unverified.
entities.reproduction_format: One purchasable digital image, advertised up to 6253 x 8940 pixels
notes: No local copy was made because the public page offers a licensed commercial asset rather than an openly reusable complete source file.

agents.name: Royal College of Physicians of London

object.description: Opening page of the English material

scope.notes: Visual comparison with Henslow's transcription confirms the opening 'Here by-gynyth þe maner of steynyng of lynne cloþ'.
The provider identifies Harley MS 2378, but supplies no folio locator; overlap with CME00012 section B is unknown.

notes: This record does not make a worldwide determination for every editorial or image component.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Four multilingual manuscript miscellanies containing English and non-English material
editorial_practices.representation: The complete English portion of manuscript A and selected English passages from manuscripts B-D
editorial_practices.description: Henslow states that he transcribed the complete English portion of manuscript A but selected from the three British Museum manuscripts.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Recipes in manuscripts B-D that repeat material already printed from manuscript A
editorial_practices.representation: Repeated items generally omitted from the later sections
editorial_practices.description: Henslow says he avoided repeating recipes from B, C, and D when they had already occurred in A, so the later sections are not complete transcriptions of their stated source ranges.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page and printed manuscript sections A-D only
editorial_practices.description: The XML encodes the title page and printed pp. 1-73 and 76-145, omitting the preface, Skeat's introduction, pp. 74-75, the plant list, index, and other book matter.

evidence.citation: CME00012 XML source in the pinned CME submodule.
evidence.locator: IDG; TEMPHEAD; TITLEPAGE; BODY/DIV1[@N='A' or @N='B' or @N='C' or @N='D']
evidence.summary: The XML identifies the item, supplies the 1899 title page, encodes four manuscript sections, and records printed and source-manuscript milestones.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00012.
evidence.summary: The item page identifies the source edition, exposes the four-part digital text, supplies medicine and materia-medica subjects, and states public-domain/No Copyright-United States status.

evidence.citation: George Henslow, Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century, with an explanatory introduction and notes by Walter W. Skeat (London: Chapman and Hall, 1899), Royal College of Physicians copy.
evidence.summary: The complete scan confirms the volume title, contributors, publication data, four printed manuscript sections, the discontiguous CME boundary, and the excluded plant list.

evidence.citation: Henslow, Medical Works of the Fourteenth Century (1899), preface and Skeat's explanatory introduction.
evidence.locator: preface, pp. iii-ix; explanatory introduction, pp. x-xv; section heads and manuscript milestones, pp. 1-145
evidence.summary: Henslow describes manuscript A's provenance and physical form, distinguishes complete-A from selected B-D transcription, explains duplicate suppression, identifies source ranges, and credits Skeat's introduction and notes.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive IIIF Presentation manifest for b24906529.
evidence.summary: The 306-canvas manifest supplies exact canvas and image-service mappings and records the 1899 date, Royal College of Physicians contribution, Internet Archive identifier, and Public Domain Mark metadata.

evidence.citation: Printed photographic specimen of Henslow manuscript A in the 1899 volume, Internet Archive leaf n6.
evidence.summary: Visual inspection confirms that the plate shows the opening 'Here by-gynyth þe maner of steynyng of lynne cloþ' at manuscript p. 26 and is only a one-page specimen.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 2378, Archives and Manuscripts catalog record 040-002032704.
evidence.summary: The current record identifies the English composite medical, culinary, and alchemical miscellany, shelfmark, extent, support, dimensions, modern foliation and older pagination, and notes that digital images are unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 2378, ff. 155r-168v, component record 041-002041400.
evidence.summary: The component record dates medical and culinary recipes to circa 1395 and identifies two recipes on ff. 157r-158r as printed by Henslow on pp. 120-122.

evidence.citation: British Library, Sloane MS 2584, Archives and Manuscripts catalog record 040-002114954.
evidence.summary: The current record identifies the 117-folio multilingual medical and recipe miscellany, describes its medieval and later components, and exposes no Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: British Library, Sloane MS 521, Archives and Manuscripts catalog record 040-002112870.
evidence.summary: The current record identifies the multilingual composite medical manuscript and its broad chronological range and exposes no Digitised Content link.
The service accepts shelfmark-and-folio requests subject to preservation and feasibility review, charges fees, photographs manuscript originals, and distinguishes supply from permission to reproduce.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: The terms warn that supplied material may involve intellectual-property rights and require written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

evidence.citation: Bridgeman Images, asset BL3708374, 'Harley MS 2378 Medical and culinary recipes, c.1395'.
evidence.summary: The provider identifies one image as British Library Harley MS 2378, advertises a maximum 6253 x 8940 file, gives no folio, and routes use through paid licensing despite a 'No Additional Copyright' label.

evidence.citation: Middle English Dictionary, bibliography/source citation for '?a1500 Henslow Recipes (Henslow)'.
evidence.summary: The MED source list assigns the Henslow Recipes source the uncertain dating ?a1500, preserving a material conflict with Henslow and Skeat's before-1400 estimate.

evidence.locator: TITLEPAGE and BODY subtree counts by DIV1/@N
evidence.summary: The XML contains four DIV1 sections, 535 P elements including six on the title page, 144 PB elements, 531 MILESTONE elements, 726 NOTE elements, two LG elements, and 37 L elements. Section C contains the substantive 33-line verse prologue; the other four lines form an editorial quotation nested in a note in section A.

open_questions.question: Where is the physical manuscript that Henslow designated manuscript A, and what is its current catalog identity and provenance after 1899?
next_steps: Trace George Henslow's estate, library, donation, and sale records and the earlier J. Johnstone provenance.
Contact relevant manuscript repositories and the Middle English Dictionary source-bibliography maintainers with the incipit, physical description, and frontispiece.
Once located, request the complete physical object rather than only the CME-selected pages.
notes: Unknown present location is not evidence that the witness is lost.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable digital facsimiles of all four physical manuscript objects be obtained?
next_steps: Locate manuscript A before pursuing its complete imaging.

Request complete-codex photography of Harley MS 2378, Sloane MS 2584, and Sloane MS 521 through British Library Digitisation Services, including exterior surfaces, endleaves, blanks, and adjacent works.
Obtain written reuse permission and record the resulting provider terms before caching any supplied files.
notes: The cached one-image manuscript-A bundle is explicitly partial, and the commercial Harley image is neither complete nor openly licensed.

open_questions.question: What are the exact modern folio locators for every passage selected by Henslow from Harley MS 2378 and Sloane MS 2584?
next_steps: Align Henslow's legacy page milestones against complete manuscript images or repository foliation concordances.
Record each discontinuous selected passage rather than reducing the selection to a continuous span.

open_questions.question: What item-specific production record explains U-M's selection of the title page and pp. 1-73 and 76-145 while omitting pp. 74-75 and the rest of the volume?
next_steps: Ask U-M Library for item-level keying specifications, source-copy identification, and selection rationale.
Compare any production documentation with the structural review notes in TEMPHEAD.
